heading: Pipeline security and incident recovery plan
label: 1208 Pipeline security and incident recovery plan

children.label: A
children.text: under severe security threat levels of alert; or

children.label: B
children.text: under specific security threat information relating to such pipeline infrastructure or operations exists; and

an incident recovery protocol plan, developed in conjunction with interstate and intrastate transmission and distribution pipeline operators and terminals and facilities operators connected to pipelines, to develop protocols to ensure the continued transportation of natural gas and hazardous liquids to essential markets and for essential public health or national defense uses in the event of an incident affecting the interstate and intrastate natural gas and hazardous liquid transmission and distribution pipeline system, which shall include protocols for restoring essential services supporting pipelines and granting access to pipeline operators for pipeline infrastructure repair, replacement, or bypass following an incident.

children.heading: Existing private and public sector efforts
children.label: b Existing private and public sector efforts
children.text: The plan shall take into account actions taken or planned by both private and public entities to address identified pipeline security issues and assess the effective integration of such actions.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: c Consultation
children.text: In developing the plan under subsection (a), the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of Transportation, interstate and intrastate transmission and distribution pipeline operators, nonprofit employee organizations representing pipeline employees, emergency responders, offerors, State pipeline safety agencies, public safety officials, and other relevant parties.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: 1 Contents
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall transmit to the appropriate congressional committees a report containing the plan required by subsection (a), including an estimate of the private and public sector costs to implement any recommendations.August 3, 2007

children.heading: Format
children.label: 2 Format
children.text: The Secretary may submit the report in both classified and redacted formats if the Secretary determines that such action is appropriate or necessary.
